abstract: This paper introduces the Network Data Repository (NR), the first comprehensive web-based platform designed for interactive graph analytics and visualization. While traditional repositories serve as static archives for downloading datasets, NR provides a dynamic ecosystem where users can analyze, explore, and visualize network data in real-time. Currently hosting over 500 graphs across 19 diverse domains—including social, biological, and technological networks—the platform enables researchers to compare global graph statistics, examine node-level features, and investigate structural connectivity through an intuitive, multi-dimensional interface. By providing tools for filtering, scaling, and zooming, NR moves beyond the limitations of static plots, allowing for the discovery of complex patterns and the verification of research claims with minimal effort. Furthermore, NR fosters scientific collaboration by integrating social features that allow the community to share visualizations, discuss findings, and contribute new datasets. Through its fusion of a large-scale data archive with powerful analytical tools, NR aims to accelerate the study of network science and improve the reproducibility of graph-based research.
